title: Card Game -- First Ace

source.file: content/A Practical Guide To Quantitative Finance Interviews Book by Xinfeng Zhou (chapters)/chapter-04-probability-theory.md

setup.narrative: A standard 52-card deck is shuffled uniformly at random. Cards are dealt one at a time face up. We observe how many cards we see UP TO AND INCLUDING the first ace.
rules: There are 4 aces in the deck and 48 non-aces.
Let X = position of the first ace (i.e., 1 if the first card is an ace).
setup.question: What is the expected position of the first ace?

learningObjectives: Decompose X = 1 + (number of non-aces before the first ace).
For each non-ace card, compute the probability it appears before all 4 aces (1/5 by symmetry).
Sum 48 indicators using linearity of expectation.

stages.label: Decompose X
stages.prompt: X = position of the first ace. Decompose X using indicators.

options.text: X = 1 + sum over the 48 non-ace cards of I(this non-ace appears before any ace).  E[X] = 1 + 48 * P(specific non-ace before all aces).

stages.explanation: X equals 1 (the ace itself) plus however many non-aces sit before it. Each non-ace contributes 1 indicator -- linearity of expectation handles the rest.

stages.label: P(specific non-ace before all aces)
stages.prompt: Consider one specific non-ace card and the 4 aces. By symmetry, where can this non-ace be relative to the 4 aces? What's P(it comes BEFORE all 4)?

options.text: 1/5.  The 4 aces partition the deck (relative to this non-ace) into 5 equally likely 'slots' (before all aces, between aces 1&2, ..., after all aces).

stages.explanation: Among the 5 random objects (4 aces + 1 specific non-ace), the non-ace is equally likely to be in any of the 5 positions. P(first) = 1/5.

stages.label: Apply linearity
stages.explanation: E[X] = 1 + 48/5 = 1 + 9.6 = 10.6. So on average the first ace appears at position 10.6 -- well before the typical 'middle' of the deck.

stages.label: Generalize
stages.prompt: For m ordinary cards and n special cards, the expected position of the first special card is:
stages.explanation: E[X] = 1 + m/(n + 1). With m = 48 (non-aces) and n = 4 (aces): 1 + 48/5 = 10.6. The intuition: each ordinary card has 1/(n + 1) chance of being before all special cards.

stages.label: Reflection
prompts: What's the expected position of the SECOND ace? (Hint: condition on the first.)
What if you only had 1 ace (Joker)? Expected position?
